rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH HEIDHUES ET AL VS. CIARA PIRON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Ciara Piron, Antoine Piron'S Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Plaintiffs' First And Seventh Causes Of Action In Plaintiffs' Original And Supplemental Complaints
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 24, 2021 line 7. Defendants Ciara Piron and Antoine Piron's Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Plaintiffs' First And Seventh Causes Of Action In Plaintiffs' Original And Supplemental Complaints is denied. Triable issues of fact exist whether defendants were substantial factor in Plaintiff's arrest. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: 2046 FILBERT, LLC VS. PEGGY BARTEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 24, 2021 line 2. INTERVENOR DAVID BARTEL's Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And For An Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs is GRANTED. Complaint, as alleged, does not state a cause of action for real properly upon which the Plaintiff has a probability of prevailing on the merits. Walgren v. Dolan (1990) 226 Cal.App.3d 572. Request for fees granted. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: EDWARD KOUNTZE ET AL VS. KALLE KARL-HEINZ PIEPER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 24, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANTS KALLE PIEPER, JOSE MONTOYA and PAOLO BROGGI's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer based on the argument that claims asserted in this action should have been brought as compulsory cross-complaint in previously filed action is OVERRULED. Claims asserted in this action do not arise out of the same transaction or series of transactions. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional distress for each plaintiff to allege facts in support of each element of this this cause of action. Demurrer to all other causes of action is otherwise sustained without leave to amend. No opposition filed as to the arguments raised in demurrer regarding these causes of action. (See Plaintiff's late-filed opposition). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
